title: Split Button
subtitle: Composite button combining a primary action with a dropdown menu of secondary actions

basic.title: Basic usage
basic.description: A split button combines a main action button with a dropdown trigger. Click the left area to trigger the main action, or the caret on the right to open the action menu.

variantsColors.title: Variants and colors
variantsColors.description: The split button supports the same NUI variants (solid, outline, ghost) and colors (primary, secondary, accent, success, info, warning, danger, neutral) as the regular button.
variantsColors.note: The divider between the main button and the trigger adapts its color automatically for each color/variant combination.

shapes.title: Shapes
shapes.description: Control the border shape of the split button. The inner corners (between main and trigger) are always straight to preserve the composite visual.
shapes.note: Shape options: 'rounded' for slightly rounded borders, 'pill' for fully rounded outer corners, and 'square' for straight borders.

sizes.title: Sizes
sizes.description: Five predefined sizes matching the NUI system. Both the main button and the trigger scale together.

raised.title: Raised
raised.description: Add a subtle shadow to elevate the split button for primary actions.

icons.title: Icons
icons.description: Use prefixIcon on the main button and triggerIcon to customize the dropdown caret.
icons.note: The default trigger icon is ri-arrow-down-s-line. You can replace it with any Remix Icon class.

loading.title: Loading state
loading.description: The loading state applies only to the main button. The dropdown trigger remains interactive unless disabled or disabledTrigger is set.
loading.note: While loading, the main button replaces its content with a spinner. The trigger remains enabled so users can still choose an alternative action from the menu.

disabled.title: Disabled state
disabled.description: Use disabled to disable the entire component, or disabledTrigger to disable only the dropdown trigger while keeping the main action clickable.
disabled.note: With disabledTrigger, the main button remains fully functional. This is useful when you want to restrict menu access while keeping the primary action available.

menuItems.title: Menu items
menuItems.description: The dropdown menu supports the same rich item features as nui-action-menu: separators, subtitles, selected state, icons and badges.

submenu.title: Submenus
submenu.description: Items with a children array render as submenu triggers that open a nested panel on hover or focus.

fullWidth.title: Full width
fullWidth.description: Use width="full" to stretch the split button to the full width of its container. Both the main button and the trigger fill the available space proportionally.

events.title: Events
events.description: Listen to onClick for the main button click, onItemAction for menu item selections, and menuOpen / menuClose for panel visibility changes.
events.consoleNote: Open DevTools (F12) and check the Console tab to see the events logged when you interact with the split button.

inputs.title: Inputs
inputs.description: Input properties to configure the split button appearance and behavior.

rows.label: Text for the main action button.
rows.prefixIcon: Remix Icon class to display before the label.
rows.color: Color palette token applied to both buttons.
rows.size: Size applied to both buttons and the divider.
rows.variant: Visual variant: solid, outline or ghost.
rows.shape: Border shape. Inner corners always remain straight.
rows.raised: Elevates the component with a subtle shadow.
rows.loading: Shows a loading spinner in the main button.
rows.disabled: Disables both the main button and the trigger.
rows.items: Array of ActionMenuItem items for the dropdown.
rows.type: HTML type attribute for the main button element.
rows.triggerIcon: Icon class for the dropdown caret button.
rows.iconSubmenu: Icon displayed on items that have children.
rows.offset: Pixel distance between trigger and the dropdown panel.
rows.offsetSubmenu: Pixel distance between a parent item and its submenu panel.
rows.itemTemplate: Custom TemplateRef to render each menu item.
rows.ariaLabel: Accessible label for the main button.
rows.triggerAriaLabel: Accessible label for the dropdown trigger button.

outputs.title: Outputs
outputs.description: Output events emitted by the split button.

rows.onClick: Emitted when the main button is clicked (not emitted if disabled or loading).
rows.onItemAction: Emitted when a menu item is selected, carrying the full ActionMenuItem.
rows.menuOpen: Emitted when the dropdown panel opens.
rows.menuClose: Emitted when the dropdown panel closes.

interface.title: Interfaces
interface.description: Type definitions used by the split button component.

colors.title: Divider color tokens
colors.description: The divider between the main button and the trigger uses per-color/variant CSS custom properties generated by the ThemeService.
colors.note: Replace {color} with any NUI color: primary, secondary, accent, success, info, warning, danger, neutral.

rows.solidDivider: Divider color for the solid variant. Defaults to a semi-transparent white.
rows.outlineDivider: Divider color for the outline variant. Defaults to a semi-transparent version of the color.
rows.ghostDivider: Divider color for the ghost variant. Defaults to a lighter alpha of the color.

structure.title: Structure tokens
structure.description: Global structure CSS custom properties that control the divider and trigger dimensions.

rows.dividerWidth: Width of the vertical divider between main button and trigger.
rows.triggerMinWidth: Minimum width of the trigger button.

roles.title: ARIA roles
roles.description: The split button exposes accurate ARIA attributes for assistive technologies.

rows.mainRole: Native button role for the main action.
rows.triggerRole: Native button role for the dropdown trigger.
rows.ariaHaspopup: Communicates that the trigger opens a menu.
rows.ariaExpanded: Reflects the open/closed state of the menu panel.
rows.panelRole: The CDK menu overlay receives role='menu'.

naming.title: Accessible naming
naming.description: Both buttons need descriptive accessible names. Use aria-label for the main button and i18n.triggerAriaLabel for the dropdown trigger.
naming.note: Icon-only or abbreviated labels should always be supplemented with an aria-label to ensure screen reader users understand each button's purpose.

keyboard.title: Keyboard navigation
keyboard.description: Powered by Angular CDK Menu for full keyboard accessibility.

rows.tab: Moves focus between the main button and the trigger.
rows.enter: Activates the focused button (main click or opens menu).
rows.arrows: Navigates between items inside the open menu.
rows.right: Opens a submenu when focused on an item with children.
rows.esc: Closes the current menu level or returns to the trigger.

setup.title: Setup
setup.description: Provide global defaults for the split button via provideNUI(). These defaults apply to every nui-split-button in the application unless overridden by a direct input.
setup.note: The global configuration is injected via Angular's DI system. Each property is optional — only the ones you specify override the library defaults.

defaults.title: Configurable properties
defaults.description: The following properties can be set globally. Each acts as a fallback when the corresponding input is not provided.

rows.offset: Global default dropdown offset in pixels.
rows.offsetSubmenu: Global default submenu offset in pixels.

priority.title: Resolution priority
priority.description: NUI resolves configuration values using the following priority chain (highest wins):
